planned_changes: In quantum_resource_4.xlsx: add a Sensitivity sheet (or extend Model) with rows that compute +5% and -5% for logical_qubits and T_depth per record, recompute phys_q and runtime_us, and compute the pass/warning/failure classification for each stress case. Reference exact Packet cell addresses in formulas.
Make all formula cells visible and ensure formulas reference Packet! cell addresses explicitly (e.g., Model!F5 = Packet!C5*1200).
Add cells that show delta comparisons used in decision logic (q_margin and t_margin_us) for each sensitivity case so reviewers can see sign flips clearly.

Update expert_memo.md: include a one-paragraph hand-check demonstrating R2 baseline -> O1 result arithmetic and one +/-5% stress recomputation; cite the exact workbook cells for baseline and sensitivity outputs.

Verify workbook parseability: open and save the .xlsx in a standard format and run an openpyxl read test (or provide a CSV export of the Sensitivity and Model sheets). Attach the CSV exports if environment parsing remains constrained.

Run a quick verification checklist before submission: (a) openpyxl read passes, (b) sensitivity rows present and recomputed classifications match memo, (c) memo cites cell addresses and includes hand-check.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Good deliverables: memo and workbook are present, traceable, and the decision logic + recommendation (O1) are well supported by the provided inspect tables. Minor gaps prevent full rubric-level signoff: the memo and workbook do not include explicit +/-5% sensitivity perturbation rows with recomputed classifications as required by the grader's rubric, and the .xlsx could not be parsed by the grader environment (openpyxl_unavailable) so formula-level verification was not performed. Addressing these will move the submission to full compliance.
